rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when they describe interface behavior; use implementation scopes for bindings involving subcomponents and connections.
Use assign statements in implementation scope only for existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use type scope for output dependency contracts.
generic_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component types for interface behavior, implementations for subcomponent bindings.
Generated AGREE code must not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the analyzed model.
Component type contracts should relate visible outputs to visible inputs; avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths.
Ensure all identifiers in AGREE annexes are explicitly visible in the current AADL scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level bindings use assign for output features or auxiliary variables.
Type-level contracts use assumptions and guarantees for interface behavior.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not in the AADL model.
Do not use assign for non-output features or auxiliary variables in implementation scope.
irrelevant_topics: Type scope range assumption and guarantee
AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
